footer_description: Codey brings GitHub admin auth, device approvals, verification mail ingest, and operational dashboards together in one place.
footer_meta: Built for verification flows, mailbox ingest, and device approvals.
home_title: Operate browser sign-in, device approvals, and verification mail from one control plane.
home_description: Codey keeps the surface small: open the admin console, inspect a device challenge, and watch verification traffic move through the same place.
home_entry_points_title: Start where your flow already lives
home_entry_admin_detail: Review runtime health, inject test data, and manage approvals.
home_entry_device_detail: Paste a user code to inspect or cross-check a pending CLI session.
home_features_title: Everything needed to operate verification flows
home_features_description: The app combines browser and CLI entry points so you can debug, approve, and monitor end-to-end verification behavior.
home_feature_browser_sign_in_description: Protect the console with GitHub-backed admin access before anyone can approve or inspect state.
home_feature_device_approvals_descriptio: Review pending CLI authorization challenges and approve or deny them from a shared dashboard.
home_feature_alias_reservations_descript: Reserve short-lived mailboxes for verification steps and keep the request context attached.
home_feature_email_ingest_description: Capture inbound messages, extracted codes, and raw payloads from the verification mailbox stream.
home_feature_sse_delivery_description: Keep the UI current with SSE-backed inbox and verification activity refreshes.
home_feature_shared_shell_description: Coordinate browser, CLI, and backend verification tasks from the same operational surface.
home_example_title: Create a reservation before the verification message arrives
home_example_description: The admin and device surfaces are useful for humans, but the underlying APIs make it easy to wire automated flows and local tooling together.
home_copy_ready: Copy the example request if you want to test the API.
home_copy_success: Copied the example request to your clipboard.
home_copy_unavailable: Clipboard access is unavailable in this browser.
home_benefits_title: A few signals you can check at a glance
home_metric_auth_modes_detail: GitHub-backed browser access and CLI-oriented device authorization share the same operational model.
home_metric_verification_path_detail: Inbox changes and parsed verification data can refresh without full page reloads.
home_metric_email_input_detail: Inbound messages can be captured, stored, previewed, and matched back to a flow.
home_process_title: Move from reservation to approval with a single console
home_process_description: The operational loop stays readable whether you're testing locally or handling live device authorizations.
home_process_step_1_description: Create a short-lived address or reservation context for the verification step.
home_process_step_2_description: Store inbound email, extract codes, or inspect the device user code provided by the CLI.
home_process_step_3_description: Approve access, review state changes, and keep follow-up actions in sync from the admin surface.
home_start_title: Open the surface you need and continue from there
home_start_description: Jump into the admin console to operate the system, or open the device inspector when a CLI session already handed you a user code.
about_description: Codey is a compact control plane for browser sign-in, CLI device flow approvals, verification mailbox ingest, and the operational data you need to keep those paths reliable.
device_description: Enter the user code shown in the CLI to inspect the current challenge and jump into the admin console if a manual approval step is needed.
device_challenge_hint_prefix: Need to act on this request?
device_empty_first: No matching device challenge was found for the current user code.
device_empty_second_prefix: If the CLI just started the flow, refresh this page or
admin_login_description: Authenticate with GitHub to review verification state, inspect incoming mail, and approve or deny device authorizations.
admin_login_access_pending_description: Your account signed in successfully, but an existing admin still needs to grant console permissions.
admin_login_capability_approve_detail: Review pending CLI challenges and confirm or deny access from the dashboard.
admin_login_capability_inspect_detail: Search inbound mail, preview payloads, and verify extracted codes.
admin_login_capability_manage_detail: Register internal OAuth clients, rotate secrets, and review supported grants.
admin_layout_controls_description: Use the top bar to switch locale and theme while you review runtime state.
admin_users_page_description: Review everyone who has signed in with GitHub, see who can reach the admin console, and grant only the permissions each person needs.
admin_users_allowlist_description: This environment is controlled by ADMIN_GITHUB_LOGINS. Allowlisted users keep full console access, while everyone else stays read-only here and loses console access on sign-in.
admin_users_empty_description: GitHub users will appear here after they sign in for the first time.
admin_users_allowlist_row_locked: Permissions are controlled by the GitHub allowlist for this environment.
admin_users_dialog_description: Choose the admin modules this person can reach. Changes take effect immediately.
admin_users_save_error_fallback: Unable to update user permissions right now.
admin_users_permission_mail_inbox_descri: View stored verification email, inspect message details, and update manual verification codes.
admin_users_permission_managed_identitie: Review shared identity summaries and update labels, plans, or lifecycle state.
admin_users_permission_cli_operations_de: Inspect active CLI connections, dispatch tasks, and handle device authorization decisions.
admin_users_permission_managed_sessions_: Review shared sessions, inspect payloads, export Codex auth JSON, and delete sessions.
admin_users_permission_oauth_clients_des: Register and update managed OAuth clients, secrets, scopes, and grant settings.
admin_users_permission_verification_doma: Manage verification email domains, enablement, and the default domain assignment.
admin_users_permission_user_access_descr: Open the user directory and grant or revoke admin access for other people.
external_services_page_description: Store service-specific settings once in Codey and let dispatched CLI tasks reuse them without separate local environment variables.
external_services_sub2api_description: Configure how Codey should sync captured Codex OAuth sessions into Sub2API.
external_services_sub2api_dispatch_hint: When this service is enabled, codex-oauth tasks dispatched from /admin/cli will fetch this Sub2API config from the app at runtime. Local CLI env vars remain an optional fallback.
external_services_field_api_key_placehol: Leave blank to keep the current API key
external_services_field_bearer_token_pla: Leave blank to keep the current bearer token
external_services_field_password_placeho: Leave blank to keep the current password
external_services_secret_keep_hint: Leave this blank to keep the currently stored secret.
external_services_secret_required_hint: Save a secret here before enabling this service.
external_services_field_default_concurre: Applied when Codey creates a new Sub2API account.
external_services_field_default_priority: Applied when Codey creates a new Sub2API account.
external_services_field_group_ids_descri: Use commas to separate one or more positive integer group IDs.
external_services_toggle_auto_fill_relat: When Codey creates a new OpenAI OAuth account in Sub2API, send Sub2API's related-model whitelist as model_mapping.
external_services_toggle_enabled_descrip: Allow Codey to send app-managed Sub2API config to dispatched codex-oauth tasks.
external_services_toggle_confirm_risk_de: Pass confirm_mixed_channel_risk when creating Sub2API accounts.
external_services_save_error: Unable to save Sub2API settings right now.
external_services_save_failed_title: Unable to save external service settings
admin_auth_required_description: Sign in with GitHub before viewing protected operational state.
admin_apps_description: Register internal clients, review supported grants, and inspect secret rotation state.
admin_apps_new_description: Configure grants, scopes, auth method, and enablement before handing the client to an internal integration.
admin_app_not_found_description: The requested managed client could not be found in the registry.
admin_app_detail_description: Update the client configuration, reveal the current secret, or rotate credentials without leaving the admin console.
admin_mail_page_description: Search stored verification mail, inspect extracted codes, and preview rendered content.
admin_cli_page_description: See your own Codey TUI clients plus shared service-client terminals that currently hold a live SSE connection to the app. If you have multiple terminals online, choose the exact connection you want to dispatch to.
admin_cli_connected_section_description: This list includes your own live TUI terminals and shared service-client terminals that are not linked to a user session. Pick a compatible connection to seed dispatch, and Codey can spread batch work across other compatible live CLIs.
admin_cli_empty_connected_title: None of your TUI terminals are connected right now
admin_cli_empty_connected_description: Start `codey` or `codey tui start` from a local terminal and it will appear here once the live connection is established.
admin_cli_flow_idle_description: Waiting for a flow dispatched from Codey web.
admin_cli_dispatch_error_fallback: Unable to dispatch the selected task.
admin_cli_dispatch_dialog_description: Queue a flow task from {cli}. Current target: {target}. Compatible live CLIs may also receive work from the same batch.
admin_cli_dispatch_dialog_idle_descripti: Choose an active TUI connection to configure and dispatch a task.
admin_cli_dispatch_flow_description: Only flows reported by this online TUI are available here.
admin_cli_dispatch_flow_section_empty: This flow does not expose any additional flow-specific parameters.
admin_cli_dispatch_string_list_hint: Use commas or new lines to enter multiple values.
admin_cli_dispatch_repeat_count_descript: Queue the same registration task this many times. Each run keeps the same overrides and follows the batch parallelism below on this TUI. Max {max}.
admin_cli_dispatch_repeat_count_error: Registration task count must be a whole number from 1 to {max}.
admin_cli_dispatch_email_batch_count_des: Each email address queues one task. {count} unique email addresses are ready to dispatch.
admin_cli_dispatch_email_batch_hint: Multiple emails will queue one task per address.
admin_cli_dispatch_email_batch_help: This field accepts multiple email addresses. You can paste lines directly or import them from a CSV file.
admin_cli_dispatch_upload_csv_empty: No email addresses were found in the selected CSV.
admin_cli_dispatch_upload_csv_error: Unable to read email addresses from the selected CSV.
admin_cli_dispatch_email_batch_identity_: Remove Identity ID before dispatching a multi-email batch.
admin_cli_dispatch_email_batch_count_err: Email batch size cannot exceed {max} addresses.
admin_cli_dispatch_email_batch_invalid: Enter at least one valid email address before dispatching a multi-email batch.
admin_cli_dispatch_parallelism_descripti: Run up to this many queued tasks at the same time. Default 1. Max {max}.
admin_cli_dispatch_parallelism_error: Parallelism must be a whole number from 1 to {max}.
admin_cli_dispatch_parallelism_count_err: Parallelism cannot exceed the queued task count ({count}).
admin_cli_flow_chatgpt_register_descript: Create a new ChatGPT account and wait for email verification.
admin_cli_flow_chatgpt_login_description: Sign in with a previously shared ChatGPT identity.
admin_cli_flow_chatgpt_team_trial_descri: Sign in and claim the ChatGPT Team free trial offer.
admin_cli_flow_chatgpt_invite_descriptio: Sign in with a shared identity and invite workspace members.
admin_cli_flow_codex_oauth_description: Complete the Codex OAuth flow and optionally create an AxonHub channel.
admin_cli_flow_noop_description: Open an empty browser page for manual inspection.
admin_cli_option_chrome_default_profile_: Reuse the local Chrome Default profile instead of an isolated temporary profile. This also keeps the browser open after the flow unless --record false is set.
admin_cli_option_headless_description: Run the browser without a visible window when possible.
admin_cli_option_slow_mo_description: Delay each browser action by a fixed number of milliseconds.
admin_cli_option_har_description: Record a HAR file for this run.
admin_cli_option_record_page_content_des: Save page.content() after the final page settles into the artifacts directory.
admin_cli_option_record_description: Keep the browser session open after the flow finishes.
admin_cli_option_restore_storage_state_d: Load a matching local ChatGPT storage state before normal login.
admin_cli_option_password_description: Override the password used by the registration flow.
admin_cli_option_verification_timeout_de: Maximum time to wait for a verification code, in milliseconds.
admin_cli_option_poll_interval_descripti: Polling interval for verification updates, in milliseconds.
admin_cli_option_identity_id_description: Use a specific shared identity by identity ID.
admin_cli_option_email_description: Use a specific shared identity by email address.
admin_cli_option_billing_name_descriptio: Name to use when the checkout billing address form asks for one.
admin_cli_option_billing_country_descrip: ISO country code for the checkout billing address, such as IE or US.
admin_cli_option_billing_address_line1_d: Street address line 1 for the checkout billing address.
admin_cli_option_billing_address_line2_d: Optional street address line 2 for the checkout billing address.
admin_cli_option_billing_city_descriptio: City or locality for the checkout billing address.
admin_cli_option_billing_state_descripti: State, province, or administrative area when required by country.
admin_cli_option_billing_postal_code_des: Postal or ZIP code for the checkout billing address.
admin_cli_option_invite_email_descriptio: One or more workspace member email addresses to invite.
admin_cli_option_invite_file_description: CSV or JSON file containing invite email addresses.
admin_cli_option_workspace_id_descriptio: Explicit OpenAI workspace ID to request during Codex OAuth.
admin_cli_option_workspace_index_descrip: 1-based workspace position to choose during Codex OAuth.
admin_cli_option_authorize_url_only_desc: Only generate the OAuth authorize URL and stop before browser login.
admin_cli_option_channel_name_descriptio: Override the AxonHub channel name for this run.
admin_flow_page_description: Review queued, running, and finished flow tasks, inspect recent runtime messages, and open the event timeline for a specific run.
admin_flow_clear_confirm_description: This removes all finished flow records currently visible on this page. Queued or running tasks are kept so active CLI work is not interrupted.
admin_flow_clear_success_partial: Cleared {count} flow records and kept {preserved} queued or running tasks.
admin_flow_clear_pending_only: There are no finished flow records to clear right now. {count} queued or running tasks are still visible.
admin_flow_clear_empty: There are no flow records to clear right now.
admin_flow_force_clear_confirm_descripti: This unconditionally removes every visible flow task on this page, including queued and running tasks. Connected CLI workers may immediately lose task state and stop reporting follow-up status.
admin_flow_force_clear_empty: There are no flow tasks to force-clear right now.
admin_flow_search_placeholder: Search flow, task ID, operator, CLI, target, or log message
admin_flow_list_description: Choose a flow task to inspect its status, connection context, and latest log timeline.
admin_flow_list_empty_description: Dispatched flow tasks will appear here once a TUI receives or starts them.
admin_flow_detail_empty_description: Pick a flow task from the list to inspect runtime status and logs.
admin_flow_runtime_description: Current task state reported by the connected TUI.
admin_flow_logs_description: Recent task lifecycle events and progress messages reported by the CLI.
admin_flow_logs_empty_description: This task has not reported any persisted lifecycle events yet.
admin_flow_payload_description: Sanitized task configuration and batch metadata captured when the run was queued.
admin_flow_not_found_description: The requested flow task could not be found, or you no longer have access to inspect it.
admin_flow_payload_empty: No additional payload metadata was stored for this task.
admin_identity_page_description: Review Codey-managed identities stored in the database, search across account metadata, and update lifecycle labels from a dedicated management page.
admin_identity_edit_description: Update the identity label used across the admin workspace.
admin_identity_edit_tags_description: Adjust the identity label and tag badges from one place.
admin_identity_save_error_fallback: Unable to update the identity right now.
admin_identity_bulk_edit_tags_descriptio: These changes apply to the identities currently selected in the table.
admin_identity_bulk_edit_scope: Apply tag changes to the selected identities: {count}.
admin_identity_bulk_copy_description: Choose which field to copy from the selected identities.
admin_identity_bulk_copy_scope: Copy values from the selected identities: {count}.
admin_identity_bulk_copy_empty: No {field} are available in the selected identities.
admin_identity_bulk_copy_download_error: Unable to download {field} as CSV right now.
admin_identity_bulk_save_error_fallback: Unable to batch update identity tags right now.
admin_identity_bulk_delete_description: This permanently removes the selected identities. Linked mail history will remain, but these identities will no longer be manageable.
admin_identity_bulk_delete_error_fallbac: Unable to delete the selected identities right now.
admin_identity_delete_confirm_descriptio: This permanently removes the identity record from the admin console. Linked mail history will remain, but the identity will no longer be manageable.
admin_identity_store_description: This summarizes whether the backing identity store is readable and where the current persisted records live.
admin_session_page_description: Review managed session snapshots synced from flows, inspect the stored payload, and delete stale session data from a dedicated management page.
admin_session_empty_description: Session snapshots will appear here after a supported flow captures and syncs a managed session payload.
admin_session_payload_description: This is the latest managed session payload captured for the selected identity.
admin_session_delete_confirm_description: This permanently removes the stored session snapshot from the admin console. The linked identity remains available.
admin_workspace_page_description: Track which invited accounts belong to which OpenAI workspace, add workspace IDs only when an account needs explicit workspace selection, and adjust linked accounts from one management page.
admin_workspace_search_placeholder: Search workspace label, workspace ID, or linked account
admin_workspace_empty_description: Run the invite flow or create a workspace here to start tracking workspace-to-account associations.
admin_workspace_dialog_description: Store the invited accounts for this OpenAI workspace. The workspace ID is optional unless an invited account belongs to multiple workspaces.
admin_workspace_id_description: Only fill this when workspace authorization must target a specific OpenAI workspace.
admin_workspace_detail_description: Review the workspace owner, linked members, and manage member invites or workspace authorization flows from one place.
admin_workspace_download_owner_and_membe: Downloaded {count} owner/member email address(es) as CSV.
Unable to download the owner/member email CSV right now.
admin_workspace_download_members_success: Downloaded {count} member email address(es) as CSV.
admin_workspace_download_members_error: Unable to download the member email CSV right now.
admin_workspace_identity_search_placehol: Search identity label, email, or ID
admin_workspace_authorization_reset_all_: Reset the stored authorization states for this workspace.
admin_workspace_authorization_reset_conf: This clears the stored Codex OAuth authorization data for the workspace owner and every member that currently has a workspace-specific authorization state.
This clears the stored Codex OAuth authorization data for {member} in this workspace.
admin_workspace_authorization_reset_erro: Unable to reset the stored authorization state right now.
admin_workspace_authorization_reset_memb: Reset the stored authorization state for {member}.
admin_workspace_codex_oauth_dispatch_hin: Codey will dispatch the Codex OAuth flow to an eligible CLI automatically and pass this workspace ID into the OAuth request. If no CLI is online, it will create a flow request instead.
admin_workspace_codex_oauth_error_fallba: Unable to start the workspace Codex OAuth flow right now.
admin_workspace_codex_oauth_requires_cli: You need CLI operations permission to start Codex OAuth from workspace details.
admin_workspace_codex_oauth_requires_own: Choose a workspace owner before starting Codex OAuth.
admin_workspace_codex_oauth_success_disp: Queued Codex OAuth on {cli} for the workspace owner.
admin_workspace_codex_oauth_success_requ: No eligible CLI was online, so Codey created a Codex OAuth request for the workspace owner.
admin_workspace_team_trial_error_fallbac: Unable to start the workspace owner Team trial flow right now.
admin_workspace_team_trial_requires_cli_: You need CLI operations permission to start the owner Team trial from workspace details.
admin_workspace_team_trial_success_reque: No eligible CLI was online, so Codey created a Team trial request for {email}.
admin_workspace_authorize_all_authorized: The workspace owner and members already have active Codex authorization.
admin_workspace_authorize_dispatch_hint: Codey will dispatch Codex OAuth tasks for the workspace owner and members to an eligible CLI automatically and pass this workspace ID into each OAuth request. If no CLI is online, it will create a flow request instead.
admin_workspace_authorize_dispatch_defau: Codey will dispatch Codex OAuth tasks for the workspace owner and members without a workspace ID so OAuth can use each account's default workspace selection. If no CLI is online, it will create a flow request instead.
admin_workspace_authorize_error_fallback: Unable to start the workspace authorization flow right now.
admin_workspace_authorize_requires_cli_p: You need CLI operations permission to start workspace authorization from workspace details.
admin_workspace_authorize_requires_membe: Add an owner or member before starting the workspace authorization flow.
admin_workspace_authorize_success_dispat: Queued authorization for {count} workspace account(s) on {cli}.
Queued authorization for {count} workspace account(s) across {cliCount} CLIs.
admin_workspace_authorize_success_reques: No eligible CLI was online, so Codey created an authorization request for {count} workspace account(s).
admin_workspace_invite_all_authorized_hi: All workspace members already have an active Codex authorization.
admin_workspace_invite_all_done_hint: All workspace members are already invited or authorized.
admin_workspace_invite_dispatch_hint: Codey will dispatch the invite flow to an eligible CLI automatically. If no CLI is online, it will create a flow request instead.
admin_workspace_invite_error_fallback: Unable to start the workspace invite flow right now.
admin_workspace_invite_requires_cli_perm: You need CLI operations permission to start invite flows from workspace details.
admin_workspace_invite_requires_members: Add at least one member before starting the invite flow.
admin_workspace_invite_requires_owner: Choose a workspace owner before starting the invite flow.
admin_workspace_invite_success_dispatche: Queued invite flow for {count} member(s) on {cli}.
Queued invite flow for {count} member(s) across {cliCount} CLIs.
admin_workspace_invite_success_requested: No eligible CLI was online, so Codey created an invite request for {count} member(s).
admin_workspace_legacy_members_notice: {count} legacy email-only member record(s) will be preserved until they are relinked.
admin_workspace_member_disabled_owner: The owner cannot also be a member.
admin_workspace_members_description: Enter one email per line or separate multiple addresses with commas.
admin_workspace_members_picker_descripti: Choose up to {max} additional identities as workspace members. A member identity can belong to multiple workspaces.
admin_workspace_member_random_confirm_de: This random selection needs to reuse {count} identities that already serve as members or owners in other workspaces. Confirm to keep them as members anyway.
admin_workspace_member_random_requires_o: Choose the workspace owner first before randomly selecting members.
admin_workspace_owner_description: Choose an identity that does not already belong to another workspace as an owner or member.
admin_workspace_owner_none_selected: Choose one identity to act as the workspace owner.
admin_workspace_owner_random_confirm_des: The randomly selected identity {identity} is already a member of {count} other workspace(s). Confirm to use it as this workspace's owner anyway.
admin_workspace_save_error_fallback: Unable to save the workspace right now.
admin_workspace_delete_confirm_descripti: This removes the stored workspace record and linked invited-account associations, then archives the owner identity so it is no longer reused.
domain_page_description: Manage the mail domains that Codey can use for verification reservations. CLI registrations will randomly pick one enabled domain for incoming mail.
domain_empty_description: Add the first verification domain and CLI registrations can start choosing from the enabled domain pool at random.
domain_field_description_placeholder: Optional note about routing or ownership.
domain_toggle_enabled_description: Disabled domains stay in the registry but cannot be assigned to new reservations.
domain_toggle_default_description: The default domain is kept only as a legacy fallback. New CLI registrations randomly choose from the enabled domains.
oauth_clients_empty_description: Create the first managed client to enable internal service auth or device flow testing.
oauth_defaults_client_credentials_detail: Service-style access can be enabled so internal tools authenticate without a browser.
oauth_defaults_device_flow_detail: Enable device authorization when a CLI or another constrained client needs human approval.
oauth_defaults_secret_detail: Client secrets are generated for you and can later be revealed or rotated from the admin console.
oauth_new_description: Define the client name, scopes, and enabled grants. You can review the generated secret immediately after creation.
oauth_new_error_create: Unable to create the OAuth client.
oauth_new_secret_panel_body: Copy this client secret now. Store it wherever the consuming integration expects it.
oauth_edit_settings_description: Adjust the managed client configuration, then save or rotate the secret when the integration needs new credentials.
oauth_edit_error_update: Unable to update the OAuth client.
oauth_edit_success_rotated: Client settings saved and a new secret was generated.
oauth_edit_reveal_error: Unable to reveal the current client secret.
oauth_field_description_placeholder: What this client is used for.
oauth_field_verification_domain_descript: Per-app domain binding is disabled. Registrations now pick a random enabled domain at reservation time.
oauth_field_allowed_scopes_description: Enter one scope per line or separate them with commas. Supported scopes: {scopes}.
oauth_toggle_enabled_description: Disable this client to prevent new tokens while keeping the definition in the registry.
oauth_toggle_client_credentials_descript: Allow server-to-server token exchange for trusted internal services.
oauth_toggle_device_flow_description: Allow a CLI or constrained client to request a user-approved device session.
oauth_grant_type_required_description: Enable client credentials, device flow, or both before saving the client.
oauth_domains_required_description: Create at least one enabled verification domain on the domains page so CLI registrations have a domain pool to choose from.
oauth_domain_default_fallback: Per-app domain binding is disabled. Codey now picks a random enabled domain each time it reserves a verification mailbox.
oauth_secret_state_description: The client secret is encrypted at rest. Reveal or rotate it only when the consuming app needs to reconfigure.
oauth_secret_panel_body: This is the current secret for the managed client. Share it only with the integration that owns this credential.
mail_inbox_stream_description: Search stored inbound messages, inspect extracted codes, and preview raw or rendered content while live updates keep the page fresh.
mail_inbox_search_placeholder: Search recipient, subject, or extracted code
mail_inbox_query_failed_description: The inbox could not be loaded right now.
mail_inbox_error_sse_unsupported: Live updates are unavailable because this browser does not support Server-Sent Events.
mail_inbox_error_stream_reconnecting: The live inbox stream disconnected. Reconnecting now.
mail_inbox_empty_description: Inbound verification mail will appear here once messages are captured.
mail_inbox_empty_filtered_title: No messages match the current filters
mail_inbox_empty_filtered_description: Try a broader search term or move back to a different page.
mail_detail_description: Inspect the stored message fields, extracted verification data, and the rendered preview.
mail_identity_archive_error: Unable to archive the linked identity right now.
mail_manual_code_description: Publish a replacement verification code for this mailbox and notify listening flows to re-enter it.
mail_manual_code_error: Unable to update the verification code.
mail_preview_empty_description: No HTML or text preview is available for this message.
mail_preview_empty_panel: There is no rendered preview for this message.
mail_content_empty: No content stored for this section.
admin_dashboard_auth_description: Sign in with GitHub before viewing protected operational state.
admin_dashboard_description_suffix: Use this console to review runtime state, inject test data, and handle approvals.
admin_dashboard_metric_identities_descri: Stored flow identities currently visible to the admin console.
admin_dashboard_metric_pending_descripti: Device challenges waiting for a decision.
admin_dashboard_metric_verification_desc: Recent verification emails, codes, and related events.
admin_dashboard_metric_notifications_des: CLI-facing notifications currently stored for downstream clients.
admin_dashboard_metric_requests_descript: Recent requests created for flow app follow-up.
admin_dashboard_code_description: Create a test code entry for a target mailbox so you can validate downstream flows quickly.
admin_dashboard_notification_description: Send a simple title/body notification to all clients or a specific target.
admin_dashboard_request_description: Track app-related follow-up work that should remain visible to the operations team.
admin_dashboard_config_description: A quick view of the runtime capabilities and backend dependencies the console expects.
admin_dashboard_config_empty_description: The backend did not return configuration data yet.
admin_dashboard_identities_description: Review database-backed identity summaries synced from flows and keep labels or lifecycle status aligned with what operators need.
admin_dashboard_identities_empty_descrip: Managed identities will appear here once a flow syncs its summary to the app database.
admin_dashboard_device_description: Approve or deny pending CLI sessions and keep an eye on the most recently updated device authorizations.
admin_dashboard_device_empty_description: New device authorizations will appear here when a CLI starts the flow.
admin_dashboard_verification_description: Recent reservation, email, and code events help you confirm the verification pipeline is healthy.
admin_dashboard_verification_empty_descr: Verification signals will appear here once inbox or code activity is recorded.
admin_dashboard_notifications_descriptio: Review messages that downstream CLI clients can consume.
admin_dashboard_notifications_empty_desc: Create a notification when you need to surface information to connected clients.
admin_dashboard_requests_description: Track pending app-related asks alongside other operational data.
admin_dashboard_requests_empty_descripti: Create a request when an operator needs to capture follow-up work.
admin_table_filtered_empty_title: No rows match the current filters
admin_table_filtered_empty_description: Adjust or clear the filters to see more results.
server_config_github_oauth_missing: GitHub browser OAuth credentials are missing.
server_config_admin_policy_allowlist: {count} GitHub logins are explicitly allowed to access the admin console.
server_config_admin_policy_bootstrap: No explicit allowlist is configured, but {count} existing admin account(s) can still sign in.
server_config_admin_policy_missing: {count} known user account(s) exist, but no admin access policy is configured.
server_config_exchange_description: Used for Microsoft or Exchange mailbox access when that ingest path is enabled.
server_config_exchange_missing: Exchange client credentials are not configured.
server_config_codex_description: Controls how Codey reaches the upstream Codex OAuth endpoints.
server_config_codex_overrides: Custom Codex OAuth environment overrides are active.
server_config_codex_defaults: Default Codex OAuth settings are in use.
server_config_oidc_description: Managed signing keys are stored and rotated for the local OIDC provider.
server_config_flow_requests_detail: Flow app requests are stored in the application database.
server_identity_store_read_error: The stored flow identity state could not be read.
server_identity_store_missing: No stored flow identity store could be found.
server_identity_store_empty: The identity store exists but does not contain any saved identities.
server_oidc_status_ready: Active key {kid}. {count} published key(s). Next rotation: {next_rotation}.
server_oidc_status_warning: Expected exactly one active signing key but found {active_count}. {count} published key(s) are still available.
oidc_device_error_invalid_code: The code you entered is incorrect. Try again.
oidc_device_error_generic: There was an error processing your request.
oidc_interaction_input_description: Enter the user code shown in the CLI to continue the standard device authorization flow.
oidc_interaction_success_body: Device authorization completed for {client}. You may close this window.
oidc_interaction_admin_required_descript: Sign in with GitHub as an admin before completing this OAuth device authorization request.
oidc_interaction_complete_body: The device authorization decision has been recorded. You may close this window.
